HELIOS - HomogEneous and Layered medIa Optical Scattering
C++ surface integral equation (SIE) code for homogeneous and layered media scattering

6#include "SingSub.h"
7#include <cmath>
8#include "GreenF.h"
9
17#define SURFTHRESH 1e-8
18
20SingSub::SingSub() {}
21
33int SingSub::Init(RWGFun* rwgIn, rvec rIn) {
34 r = rIn;
35
36 // Check if triangle has changed, only then recompute edge normals
37 if (rwgIn != rwgFun) {
38 rwgFun = rwgIn;
39 edgeNormals.clear();
40
41 // Edge normals: e_i = (v_{i+1} - v_i) x n_ref, normalized
42 edgeNormals.push_back(cross(
43 (rvec)(rwgFun->TrianglePtr()->Node(1) - rwgFun->TrianglePtr()->Node(0)),
44 rwgFun->TrianglePtr()->RefNormalVector()));
45 edgeNormals.push_back(cross(
46 (rvec)(rwgFun->TrianglePtr()->Node(2) - rwgFun->TrianglePtr()->Node(1)),
47 rwgFun->TrianglePtr()->RefNormalVector()));
48 edgeNormals.push_back(cross(
49 (rvec)(rwgFun->TrianglePtr()->Node(0) - rwgFun->TrianglePtr()->Node(2)),
50 rwgFun->TrianglePtr()->RefNormalVector()));
51
52 // Normalize
53 for (int i = 0; i < 3; i++)
54 edgeNormals[i] /= sqrt(dot(edgeNormals[i], edgeNormals[i]));
55 }
56
57 // Calculate "t" factors
58 t.clear();
59 for (int i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
60 t.push_back(
61 dot(edgeNormals[i], (rvec)(r - rwgFun->TrianglePtr()->Node(i))));
62 }
63
64 // Calculate h and omega
65 h = dot(rwgFun->TrianglePtr()->RefNormalVector(),
66 (rvec)(r - rwgFun->TrianglePtr()->Node(0)));
67
68 // Auxiliary unit vectors from r to vertices
69 rvec a1, a2, a3;
70 double fNorm;
71
72 a1 = rwgFun->TrianglePtr()->Node(0) - r;
73 fNorm = sqrt(dot(a1, a1));
74 if (fNorm > 0) a1 /= fNorm;
75
76 a2 = rwgFun->TrianglePtr()->Node(1) - r;
77 fNorm = sqrt(dot(a2, a2));
78 if (fNorm > 0) a2 /= fNorm;
79
80 a3 = rwgFun->TrianglePtr()->Node(2) - r;
81 fNorm = sqrt(dot(a3, a3));
82 if (fNorm > 0) a3 /= fNorm;
83
84 double x = 1. + dot(a1, a2) + dot(a1, a3) + dot(a2, a3);
85 double y = fabs(dot(a1, cross(a2, a3)));
86
87 if (h > 0.)
88 omega = 2. * fabs(atan2(y, x));
89 else
90 omega = -2. * fabs(atan2(y, x));
91 return 0;
92}
93
103int SingSub::SetTriangleRWG(RWGFun* rwgIn, rvec rIn) {
104 r = rIn;
105 rwgFun = rwgIn;
106 tPtr = rwgFun->TrianglePtr();
107
108 // Signed distance to plane
109 h = dot(tPtr->RefNormalVector(), (rvec)(r - tPtr->Node(0)));
110
111 // Build unit edge tangents and corresponding edge normals
112 double fNorm;
113 rvec edge[3], a[3], anorm[3];
114 for (int i = 0; i < 3; ++i) {
115 int ii = (i + 1) % 3;
116 edge[i] = tPtr->Node(ii) - tPtr->Node(i);
117 edge[i] /= sqrt(dot(edge[i], edge[i]));
118 eNar[i] = cross(edge[i], tPtr->RefNormalVector());
119 eNar[i] /= sqrt(dot(eNar[i], eNar[i]));
120
121 a[i] = tPtr->Node(i) - r;
122 fNorm = sqrt(dot(a[i], a[i]));
123 if (fNorm > 0)
124 anorm[i] = a[i] / fNorm;
125 else
126 anorm[i] = a[i];
127
128 tar[i] = -dot(eNar[i], a[i]);
129 }
130
131 // Signed solid angle using unit vectors anorm[i]
132 double x = 1. + dot(anorm[0], anorm[1]) + dot(anorm[1], anorm[2]) +
133 dot(anorm[2], anorm[0]);
134 double y = fabs(dot(anorm[0], cross(anorm[1], anorm[2])));
135
136 if (h > 0.) {
137 omega = 2. * fabs(atan2(y, x));
138 } else {
139 omega = -2. * fabs(atan2(y, x));
140 }
141
142 // Precompute line integrals on each edge for q = {-1,1,3}
143 for (int i = 0; i < 3; ++i) {
144 int ii = (i + 1) % 3;
145 double sp(dot(edge[i], a[ii]));
146 double sm(dot(edge[i], a[i]));
147
148 double Rp(sqrt(dot(a[ii], a[ii])));
149 double Rm(sqrt(dot(a[i], a[i])));
150 double R0(Rp * Rp - sp * sp);
151
152 if (R0 < 0.) R0 = 0.;
153
154 R0 = sqrt(R0);
155
156 // Log term with endpoint handling (Rp==0 or Rm==0)
157 double fLog;
158 if (Rp == 0 || Rm == 0)
159 fLog = 0.;
160 else if (Rm + sm > Rp - sp)
161 fLog = log((Rp + sp) / (Rm + sm));
162 else
163 fLog = log((Rm - sm) / (Rp - sp));
164
165 if (fabs(R0) < SURFTHRESH) {
166 ILar[i][0] = 0;
167 ILar[i][1] = (sp * Rp - sm * Rm) / 2.;
168 ILar[i][2] = (sp * Rp * Rp * Rp - sm * Rm * Rm * Rm) / 4.;
169 } else {
170 ILar[i][0] = fLog;
171 ILar[i][1] = (R0 * R0 * fLog + sp * Rp - sm * Rm) / 2.;
172 ILar[i][2] = R0 * R0 * 3. / 8. * (R0 * R0 * fLog + sp * Rp - sm * Rm) +
173 (sp * Rp * Rp * Rp - sm * Rm * Rm * Rm) / 4.;
174 }
175 }
176
177 // Precompute surface integrals ISar[q]
178 ISar[0] = omega / h;
179 ISar[1] = omega * h;
180 ISar[2] = omega * h * h * h;
181 ISar[3] = omega * h * h * h * h * h;
182 for (int i = 0; i < 3; ++i) {
183 if (fabs(tar[i]) > SURFTHRESH) {
184 ISar[1] += tar[i] * ILar[i][0];
185 ISar[2] += tar[i] * (h * h * ILar[i][0] + ILar[i][1]);
186 ISar[3] += tar[i] *
187 (h * h * h * h * ILar[i][0] + h * h * ILar[i][1] + ILar[i][2]);
188 }
189 }
190 ISar[1] /= -1;
191 ISar[2] /= -3;
192 ISar[3] /= -5;
193
194 return 0;
195}
196
197// Change only the RWG/triangle while keeping r fixed.
198int SingSub::ChangeTriangleRWG(RWGFun* rwgIn) {
199 rwgFun = rwgIn;
200 return 0;
201}
202
203// Scalar K1 term
204double SingSub::K1(int q) { return ISar[(q + 3) / 2] * rwgFun->RWGPreFactor(); }
205
206// Vector K2 term
207rvec SingSub::K2(int q) {
208 rvec rho(r - h * tPtr->RefNormalVector());
209 rvec result((rho - rwgFun->FreeVertex()) * ISar[(q + 3) / 2]);
210 for (int i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
211 result += eNar[i] * ILar[i][(q + 3) / 2] / (q + 2.);
212 }
213 result *= rwgFun->RWGPreFactor() / 2.;
214 return result;
215}
216
217// Vector K3 term
218rvec SingSub::K3(int q) {
219 rvec result(0., 0., 0.);
220 for (int i = 0; i < 3; i++) result += eNar[i] * ILar[i][(q + 1) / 2];
221 if (q == -1)
222 result += tPtr->RefNormalVector() * omega;
223 else
224 result -= h * q * tPtr->RefNormalVector() * ISar[(q + 1) / 2];
225 return result;
226}
227
228// Normal-only part of K3(q)
229rvec SingSub::K3n(int q) {
230 if (q == -1)
231 return tPtr->RefNormalVector() * omega;
232 else
233 return -h * q * tPtr->RefNormalVector() * ISar[(q + 1) / 2];
234}
235
236// Vector K4 term
237rvec SingSub::K4(int q) {
238 return cross((rvec)(r - rwgFun->FreeVertex()), K3(q)) *
239 rwgFun->RWGPreFactor() / (-2.);
240}
241
250double SingSub::IL(int q, int triEdgeIndex) {
251 rvec p1(rwgFun->TrianglePtr()->Node(triEdgeIndex % 3));
252 rvec p2(rwgFun->TrianglePtr()->Node((triEdgeIndex + 1) % 3));
253 rvec vTemp(p2 - p1);
254 rvec s(vTemp / sqrt(dot(vTemp, vTemp)));
255 double sp(dot(s, (rvec)(p2 - r)));
256 double sm(dot(s, (rvec)(p1 - r)));
257
258 vTemp = r - p2;
259 double Rp(sqrt(dot(vTemp, vTemp)));
260 vTemp = r - p1;
261 double Rm(sqrt(dot(vTemp, vTemp)));
262 double R0(Rp * Rp - sp * sp);
263
264 if (R0 < 0.) R0 = 0.;
265
266 R0 = sqrt(R0);
267
268 double fLog;
269 // Note: this is not strictly correct! For r on either p1 or p2,
270 // IL(-1) will diverge logarithmically. However, in the singularity
271 // subtraction technique only t_i * IL(-1) appears, the result will
272 // be equal to zero and so this is acceptable. Also, for q > -1,
273 // fLog is multiplied by R0^2, which is equal to zero for these
274 // cases, so again, this is acceptable:
275 if (Rp == 0 || Rm == 0)
276 fLog = 0.;
277 else if (Rm + sm > Rp - sp)
278 fLog = log((Rp + sp) / (Rm + sm));
279 else
280 fLog = log((Rm - sm) / (Rp - sp));
281
282 switch (q) {
283 case -1:
284 return fLog;
285 case 1:
286 if (fabs(R0) < SURFTHRESH)
287 return (sp * Rp - sm * Rm) / 2.;
288 else
289 return (R0 * R0 * fLog + sp * Rp - sm * Rm) / 2.;
290 case 3:
291 if (fabs(R0) < SURFTHRESH)
292 return (sp * Rp * Rp * Rp - sm * Rm * Rm * Rm) / 4.;
293 else
294 return R0 * R0 * 3. / 8. * (R0 * R0 * fLog + sp * Rp - sm * Rm) +
295 (sp * Rp * Rp * Rp - sm * Rm * Rm * Rm) / 4.;
296 }
297 return 0.;
298}
299
300// Exact surface integral of @f$ R^q @f$ over the triangle.
301double SingSub::IS(int q) {
302 double result(0);
303 switch (q) {
304 case -3:
305 result = omega / h;
306 break;
307 case -1:
308 result = h * omega;
309 for (int i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
310 if (fabs(t[i]) > SURFTHRESH) result += t[i] * IL(-1, i);
311 }
312 result *= -1.;
313 break;
314 case 1:
315 result = h * h * h * omega;
316 for (int i = 0; i < 3; i++)
317 if (fabs(t[i]) > SURFTHRESH)
318 result += t[i] * (h * h * IL(-1, i) + IL(1, i));
319 result /= -3.;
320 break;
321 case 3:
322 result = h * h * h * h * h * omega;
323 for (int i = 0; i < 3; i++)
324 if (fabs(t[i]) > SURFTHRESH)
325 result +=
326 t[i] * (h * h * h * h * IL(-1, i) + h * h * IL(1, i) + IL(3, i));
327 result /= -5.;
328 }
329 return result;
330}

#define SURFTHRESH
Numerical threshold for detecting points lying near an edge or plane.
